channel.title: Adhoc on adoption curve dot net
channel.description: Recent content in Adhoc on adoption curve dot net

item.title: Getting an iPhone's UUID number for ad-hoc app deployment
item.description: Deploying iPhone applications to testers involves creating an ad-hoc distribution package that they can install through iTunes, rather than the App Store. To do this, you need to get each iPhone’s identifier or UUID code. There are two steps to this - the first is to get your guinea pig to connect their iPhone and fire up iTunes. Then on the iPhone Summary page, they’ll see their phone’s serial number, like this:
